claim: Prediction in atom ATOM_T005_S062_PRE_02 ("I think that can work, but the sample efficiency there is pretty terrible. Yo...") holds if and only if the first-principle constraint in atom ATOM_T013_S019_FIR_01 ("fact that it is in principle possible to automate this work. And there should...") is binding.
first_principles_validity_hypothesis: The prediction "I think that can work, but the sample efficiency there is..." is forced by the underlying constraint "fact that it is in principle possible to automate this wo..."; if the constraint is not binding the prediction may still hold by accident but the binding case is the load-bearing version of this candidate.
why_novel_vs_speaker: The speaker introduces the prediction (snippet S062) and the first-principle (snippet S019) in separate contexts; the speaker does not state that the second forces the first.
why_potentially_useful: A prediction that is forced by a first-principle is more falsifiable than a free-floating prediction: if the principle holds and the prediction fails, one of them is wrong, which is a binary research question.
